steps.title: Identify the subject and structural requirement
steps.content: The subject is “the Continental Army's difficulty in procuring supplies” (singular). Because this is a that-clause, it requires a finite verb between the subject and “due to concerns...”.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: A finite verb must agree with its subject in number. C uses the singular auxiliary “is” with “occurring,” which correctly agrees with the singular subject “difficulty.” A and D use plural forms that violate agreement, while B provides only a participle without a finite verb, which cannot be the main verb of a clause. That matches choice C — C is correct.
